steps.title: Identify the introductory phrase after the closing quote
steps.content: After the closing quotation mark following direction, the next words are “in 1909, for instance”. This phrase sets the time and signals an illustration. It introduces the independent clause “judges recognized Selma Ottilia Lovisa Lagerlöf...”

steps.title: Apply the comma rule for introductory phrases
steps.content: An introductory phrase must be separated from the independent clause it precedes by a comma, placed directly after the closing quotation mark. The choice direction,” correctly follows this pattern. Choices A, C, and D each fail—A uses a semicolon (which joins two independent clauses, not a phrase to a clause), C omits necessary punctuation, and D adds extraneous words. That matches choice B — B is correct.
